message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Tac/Quila (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 2,900+ reviews; OpenTable 3,000+ reviews (combined 5,000+ across major platforms)
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Opened 2019 by husband-and-wife owners Liz and Mark Ostoich (former attorneys; also operate FARM in downtown Palm Springs); ownership stable through 2026
- Credentials: ["Michelin Guide California recommended (2022)","Michelin Guide California recommended (2023)","Michelin Guide recognition continued through 2024-2025 per restaurant's own badge display [UNVERIFIED — confirmed on site, not independently cross-checked on guide.michelin.com which returned 403]","OpenTable Diners' Choice","Featured in Palm Springs Life ('Dressed for the Part')","Listed by Visit Greater Palm Springs and Visit Palm Springs as a featured dining experience"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly on 'best Mexican restaurant Palm Springs' (top result, often #1), 'tequila mezcal bar Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs Mexican', and 'Michelin Palm Springs'. Does NOT reliably surface on broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining Palm Desert' queries — those are dominated by Workshop Kitchen & Bar, Morgan's in the Desert, Le Vallauris, Chez Pierre, Edge Steakhouse.
- Site structured data: tacquila.com is built on the SpotHopper platform. No schema.org JSON-LD or microdata markup detected in the HTML. Hours, address, and menu links are present in plain HTML but not machine-readable as Restaurant schema. Menu is split across separate pages (/palm-springs-tac-quila-food-menu, /palm-springs-tac-quila-drink-menu) without structured MenuItem markup. This is a real gap for agent ingestion despite strong third-party signal.
- Visibility gap score: 5
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tac/Quila and its Michelin Guide recognition:

Tac/Quila is listed in both the MICHELIN Guide California 2022 and MICHELIN Guide California 2023. The restaurant earned its first major accolade in 2022 with inclusion in the Michelin Guide California as a recommended restaurant, recognizing its modern Mexican cuisine.

Tac/Quila is a Mexican restaurant located in downtown Palm Springs, California, specializing in authentic cuisine featuring in-house made tortillas, street-style tacos, and an extensive selection of agave spirits, situated at 415 North Palm Canyon Drive, and opened its doors in 2019.

Eight Palm Springs eateries are listed in the Michelin Guide, including Tac/Quila, making it a notable recognition for the restaurant's excellence in the local dining scene.

Links: [{"title":"Tac/Quila - About","url":"https://tacquila.com/palm-springs-tac-quila-about"},{"title":"Dressed for the Part | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/tac-quila-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Tac/Quila - Palm Springs","url":"https://www.palmsprings.com/tac-quila/"},{"title":"Tac/Quila — Grokipedia","url":"https://grokipedia.com/page/tacquila"},{"title":"TAC/QUILA - Updated June 2026 - 4418 Photos & 2942 Reviews - 415 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Tapas/Small Plates - +17604174471 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/tac-quila-palm-springs"},{"title":"Tac/Quila - Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://tacquila.com/"},{"title":"Restaurant News Bites: A Shorter Restaurant Week Returns on May 31; Introducing Tac/Quila; and a Whole Lot More! - Coachella Valley Independent","url":"https://cvindependent.com/2019/04/restaurant-news-bites-a-shorter-restaurant-week-returns-on-may-31-introducing-tac-quila-and-a-whole-lot-more/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Tac/Quila in Palm Springs and its owners:

## Owners

Tac/Quila is the brainchild of husband-and-wife duo Liz and Mark Ostoich. Liz Ostoich, who formerly made her living as an attorney, co-owns Tac/Quila with her still-practicing attorney husband Mark.

## Background and Connection to FARM

The couple took a weekend break from their law careers and decided to dream about opening a restaurant, brainstorming and reflecting on where their dreams intersected. They discovered their life change would involve hospitality, as they love people and their stories. Their first restaurant venture was FARM, also located in downtown Palm Springs.

## Restaurant Details

Opened in 2019, Tac/Quila is located at 415 North Palm Canyon Drive and quickly gained a reputation for its creative approach to traditional Mexican dishes, blending authentic flavors with a modern twist. Tac/Quila is their made-up word for combining gourmet Jalisco style cuisine with specialty tequilas and mezcals.

I've completed the web search for "Tac/Quila" Michelin Guide 2024 OR 2025 Palm Springs. Here are the results:

## Key Findings:

Tac/Quila is a restaurant in the 2025 MICHELIN Guide USA. The restaurant is located in downtown Palm Springs at 415 North Palm Canyon Drive.

### About the Restaurant:

Tac/Quila opened its doors in 2019 as a vibrant hacienda-inspired venue that blends upscale and casual dining experiences. Founded by Liz and Mark Ostoich, a couple of former lawyers who pivoted to the hospitality industry, Tac/Quila emphasizes fresh, lively ingredients in dishes like carne asada tacos, al pastor, lobster specialties, and inventive cocktails such as margarita flights.

### Menu & Cuisine:

Tac/Quila's cuisine reflects the diversity of Jalisco, drawing from the state's south, east, and western regions, incorporating coastal and inland recipes. The tortillas are made in house, the flavors are lively and the portions are generous, and cheery service will help you select from a bountiful list of agave spirits.

### Recognition:

As the only Mexican-focused establishment in Palm Springs to receive this distinction among the city's limited Michelin selections, it underscores the restaurant's innovative approach to traditional Jalisco-style dishes. Tac/Quila has also been awarded TripAdvisor's Travelers' Choice designation in 2025, placing it in the top 10% of restaurants worldwide.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "FARM Palm Springs restaurant Liz Ostoich owner downtown"

Links: [{"title":"FARM - Palm Springs","url":"https://www.palmsprings.com/farm/"},{"title":"How Liz Ostoich Brings France to Downtown Palm Springs | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/how-liz-ostoich-brings-france-to-downtown-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Show 428, June 5, 2021: Liz Ostoich, Owner, Farm, La Plaza, Palm Springs Part Two - SoCal Restaurant Show","url":"https://socalrestaurantshow.com/media/podcasts/show-428-june-5-2021-liz-ostoich-owner-farm-la-plaza-palm-springs-part-two/"},{"title":"Farm | Traditional French Dishes | Palm Springs","url":"https://www.farmpalmsprings.net/"},{"title":"Farm - goop","url":"https://goop.com/place/california/palm-springs/baristo-restaurants/farm/"},{"title":"FARM - A little slice of the South of France in Palm Springs","url":"https://www.winnyat.com/post/farm-a-little-slice-of-the-south-of-france-in-palm-springs"},{"title":"Clandestino","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/clandestino/675/"},{"title":"Tac/Quila - About","url":"https://tacquila.com/palm-springs-tac-quila-about"},{"title":"Food For Thought - DAP Health","url":"https://daphealth.org/food-for-thought/"}]

I found information about FARM, the Palm Springs restaurant. Here are the key details:

**About FARM:**
FARM was founded by Liz Ostoich, a passionate restaurateur with a deep love for the flavors and traditions of French country cooking. FARM opened its doors in 2016 in the charming La Plaza courtyard of downtown Palm Springs.

**Restaurant Concept:**
Ostoich wanted to create a place that transported guests to the quaint villages of Provence, where the pace is slower, the ingredients are fresh, and every meal is a celebration. FARM transports diners straight to the French countryside with its enchanting ambiance, rustic décor, and an authentic Provençal menu.

**Background on Liz Ostoich:**
Ostoich had been a land-use attorney, but never enjoyed what she did as a lawyer. Ostoich modeled Farm after the places she visited in her travels through southern France.

**Additional Ventures:**
FARM became a smashing success that spawned similar triumphs in 2019's Michelin Guide-recommended Tac/Quila, and 2022's The Front Porch and Clandestino.

I found search results for "Tac/Quila" Palm Springs Life "Dressed for the Part". Here's what I discovered:

The first result is from Palm Springs Life magazine, featuring an article titled "Dressed for the Part" about Tac/Quila restaurant. Here are the key highlights:

**Restaurant Overview:**
Tac/Quila is a mashup of tacos and tequila, and it's located at 415 N. Palm Canyon Drive, Palm Springs, with hours from 11 a.m. to 10 p.m. daily.

**Ambiance:**
The dog-friendly portico greeting guests is bright, breezy, and softly lit at night by hanging gas lanterns, while white marble tables with comfy chairs juxtapose the lush greenery and exotic flowers bursting forth from the living wall. Splashy red banquette booths facing green-cushioned chairs are the star of the interior.

**Ownership & Background:**
Liz Ostoich, who formerly made her living as an attorney, co-owns Tac/Quila with her still-practicing attorney husband Mark. Ostoich's travels through Jalisco, Mexico, were the inspiration for Tac/Quila.

**Menu & Drinks:**
Recommended dishes include avocado bites, nachos, carne asada, short rib or lobster tacos, and Tac/Quila serves over 45 different tequilas and 16 mezcals.

content.text: I have sufficient verification. Let me check one nuance — the claim says owners "also operate FARM" but evidence suggests they also operate The Front Porch and Clandestino too, which is additive not contradictory. Also the Michelin first-recognition year: per Descanso Resort article and Palm Springs Life, Tac/Quila is in 2025 guide; the 2022 claim is supported by the first search result. Let me also verify the 2022/2023 distinction more carefully.

Based on the search results, I found information about the Michelin Guide California 2022 inaugural Palm Springs recommendations:

The Michelin Guide California touched down in Palm Springs for the first time in 2022. The inaugural Palm Springs guide includes eight eateries: 4 Saints, Bar Cecil, Boozehounds, Cheeky's, Colony Club, Tac/Quila, The Barn Kitchen at Sparrows Lodge and Workshop Kitchen & Bar.

Regarding Tac/Quila specifically, Tac/Quila offers a menu that bridges traditional Mexican fare with luxe ingredients, with street taco options including carne asada, cochinita pibil, and crispy Baja-style fish, while elevated plates like lobster enchiladas and ahi tuna tostadas add sophistication. The hacienda-inspired venue features both street tacos with typical fillings like carne asada or al pastor, as well as more upscale options like lobster and seared ahi tuna.

content.text: All major claims are verified. Submitting findings.

confirms: Opened 2019 by husband-and-wife owners Liz and Mark Ostoich (both former/practicing attorneys) — confirmed via Palm Springs Life, Coachella Valley Independent, and tacquila.com About page
Owners also operate FARM in downtown Palm Springs (opened 2016 in La Plaza courtyard) — confirmed via Palm Springs Life and farmpalmsprings.net

Michelin Guide California recommended 2022 (inaugural Palm Springs list of 8 restaurants, alongside Workshop Kitchen & Bar, Bar Cecil, etc.) — confirmed via Michelin Guide USA listing and multiple press sources
Michelin Guide recognition continued through 2025 — confirmed via Descanso Resort July 2025 article and Palm Springs Life
Yelp review count 2,900+ (currently 2,942) — confirmed via Yelp listing snippet
OpenTable review count 3,000+ (currently ~3,005, 4.8 stars) — confirmed via OpenTable
Featured in Palm Springs Life 'Dressed for the Part' article — confirmed
Listed by Visit Palm Springs and Visit Greater Palm Springs — confirmed via direct listings
Address 415 N. Palm Canyon Drive, Palm Springs — confirmed
contradicts: Owner portfolio is understated — Liz Ostoich also operates The Front Porch and Clandestino (both 2022 openings), not only FARM. This doesn't refute the FARM claim but the brief should reflect a 4-concept hospitality group, not a 2-concept one.
flagged_as_unverified: Specific 2023 Michelin Guide California listing — Michelin's site returned 403 to direct fetch; the 2022 inaugural and 2025 continuation are independently sourced, but year-by-year continuity through 2023 and 2024 specifically was not separately confirmed from primary Michelin pages (only via secondary aggregators that lump 'continues to be recognized')
OpenTable Diners' Choice award specifically — high ratings/booking volume confirmed but no independent source surfaced the literal 'Diners' Choice' designation; could not confirm or refute
input.notes: Core credential and history claims hold up well under independent search. Michelin 2022 inaugural inclusion is firmly documented across multiple sources (Visit California, Palm Springs Life, Michelin Guide article on Palm Springs ceremony). 2025 continuation also confirmed. Ownership story (Liz and Mark Ostoich, ex-attorneys, FARM operators) is well-sourced. Minor expansion needed: the Ostoich group also runs The Front Porch and Clandestino as of 2022. Review counts match current platform numbers. Michelin's 403 to direct fetch is a known anti-bot behavior, not evidence against the listing — restaurant page exists in search index and is corroborated by multiple secondary sources naming the 8 inaugural Palm Springs restaurants. Diners' Choice award specifically is the weakest single claim.
